title: Rich Australian Vegetable Tart
description: A charred and velvety Australian dish featuring creme-patissiere, this tart is perfect for adventurous eaters, made with a variety of roasted vegetables and a flaky crust, served on a lazy-susan-spinner for a fun and interactive dining experience.
instructions: Roll out puff pastry on a floured surface to a thickness of about 1/8 inch.
In a large bowl, toss sliced eggplant, zucchini, and red bell pepper with olive oil, salt, and pepper until they are evenly coated.
Arrange the vegetables on the pastry, leaving a 1 inch border around the edges.
Spread creme-patissiere over the vegetables, leaving a small border around the edges.
Fold the edges of the pastry up over the filling, pressing gently to seal.
Brush the pastry with olive oil and sprinkle with chopped thyme.
Place the tart on a lazy-susan-spinner and bake for 40 minutes, or until the pastry is golden brown and the vegetables are tender.
Remove the tart from the oven and let it cool on a wire rack for 10 minutes.
Shock the tart in an ice bath to stop the cooking process and give it a velvety texture.
